Review_Title: said I had to pay for that seat'
Review: The low fare they give is not worth the hassle of waiting in long check-in lines, departure delays and poor customer service.  Ihad arrived at the airport two hours early anticipating that there would be a decent-sized line but that it would only take 30 minutes maximum to check-in my baggage and get my boarding pass.  The line to check-in extended all the way from the counter to the entrance doorss. Due to the slow process of check-in, I had only an hour left before my flight was set to depart and I had to summon a member of the AirAsia staff to expedite my check-in. When I got to the counter after bypassing the line, I was informed by the agent that my flight had been delayed and that I would have to pay a fee because my luggage was more than the 15kg allowance I had paid for.  I was informed that they were charging me 2,799.98 THB (90.58 USD) in addition to the 385.20 THB (12.24 USD) I paid for my 15kg baggage allowance.  In total, my baggage fees amounted to 3,185.18 THB (101.29 USD). Once I paid the fee for my baggage, I proceeded through security to find out our flight was delayed.  After we boarded the plane, I saw that there were a couple emergency exit row seats available and asked one of the flight attendants if I could have that seat. They denied me the upgrade and said I had to pay for that seat.

Review: Kuala Lumpur to Yogyakarta. I did not check in online or select a seat, but this time, my travel party and I were not scattered apart; we were seated in adjacent rows, still with nobody in the middle seat. The check-in process was wholly digital and automated, but the staff managing crowd control and directing passengers made the check-in experience feel like enlisting for a military boot camp. However boarding was orderly and efficient, with slowdowns again tied to the capacity of the plane and some passengers in the earlier zones boarding with the later. The boarding gate staff members were friendlier, albeit still on the colder side, and once on board, I was welcomed enthusiastically by the lead flight attendant. Despite finishing boarding early, we pushed back and took off 40-ish minutes late due to a last-minute no-show. Soon after, the service started, and all of the crews were nice, warm, cheerful, and energetic. There was no Wi-Fi due to this plane being an ex-MYAirline plane, but I kinda wished that all AirAsia planes were like this; seats slimline and thin but well-padded, decently pre-reclined, and easy to use, and the cabin so pristine, well air conditioned, and clean that I had no idea this plane was 16 years old.  Even though I was in the hot seat, the slimline design of the seats gave me 1"-2" extra pitch. Landing was not terribly late, and we only needed to wait a while for the baggage to arrive. It's amazing how AirAsia turned an uneventful flight into something memorable, and this time, I can see their real potential as the world's best low-cost airline.

Review: Yogyakarta to Kuala Lumpur on their 186-seater Airbus A320-200. As I did not prepay for a seat, my travel parties were scattered everywhere in the cabin from rows 16 to 32, but by sheer luck, none of us were assigned the middle seat. Everybody working the front line at YIA was friendly, efficient, and accommodating, and most importantly, they stood their ground, resulting in a very orderly boarding process; the slowdowns only occurred due to the plane's capacity and the passengers who didn't hear their zone being called, and, as a result, boarded late. Takeoff was obviously late due to the late arrival of this aircraft, but soon after, the services began. The cabin was clean, comfortable, and in great condition, considering the airframe being almost 7.5 years old, with the only minus point being a minor coffee spillover in one of the window sheds. The seat was well-padded and generally comfortable, with the only minus point being the thick design, which made the legroom very tight. The cabin crews serving me on this flight were mixed in friendliness, with most of them leaning on the "friendly, attentive, and courteous but cold in body language" side. There was a Wi-Fi entertainment onboard, but I couldn't connect without figuring out what the captive portal was. We landed not terribly behind schedule, and our bags came out not a minute after we passed immigration. Overall, I can see why they are the best in the low-cost airline category, and with just a bit more energy on the crew's side, this flight would have been a 10/10.

Review: I have never in my life encountered a company that treats paying customers with such utter contempt. My recent flight with AirAsia from Hong Kong to Kuala Lumpur was not just disappointing  it was a masterclass in incompetence, arrogance, and corporate greed. Lets start with the delay. The flight, scheduled for 6:15 PM, did not leave the tarmac until 8:33 PM. Thats almost two and a half hours late. They did mention that the flight was delayed by 30 minutes, but it turned into a 2 hour+ long ordeal. But unbelievably, that wasnt even the low point. The real disgrace came during check-in. Our carry-on bag was perhaps 1kg over the limit, and instead of handling it professionally, the AirAsia staff member decided to turn it into a petty power trip. When we said wed simply remove a few items to meet the requirement, this employee threatened us three separate times  yes, three  saying, You can do that, but if any of my colleagues catch you, they WILL fine you HKD 1,000 per kilo. The tone was spiteful, smug, and downright demeaning. It was like being spoken to by a petty tyrant rather than a customer service representative. We werent breaking the law  we were passengers trying to comply. Instead, we were treated like criminals. Let me be clear: I understand airlines need to enforce baggage policies. But there is a chasm between professionalism and predation  and AirAsia is firmly on the wrong side of it. This was not customer service; this was harassment thinly disguised as policy enforcement. To make matters worse, we had even paid for the Value Pack, which includes perks like pre-selected seats, onboard meals, and a 20kg checked baggage allowance. This wasnt a free flight. We paid, we followed the rules, and we were still publicly humiliated and threatened for a trivial, fixable issue. AirAsias entire business model appears to be built on nickel-and-diming passengers while stripping away every ounce of dignity and decency. Its one thing to be a low-cost airline  its another to operate with low-cost ethics. After this ordeal, I can say with absolute certainty: I will never step foot on an AirAsia flight again. I would rather pay double, even triple, the price on another airline than endure another second of this degrading nonsense. AirAsia should be ashamed  not just for their incompetence, but for the way they make their customers feel: belittled, powerless, and disrespected. This airline is the definition of cheap in every possible sense of the word. Avoid AirAsia at all costs. No amount of savings is worth the humiliation.

Review: Air Asia excels in consistency - consistently delayed flights & poor service. I try my best to avoid flying on Air Asia but on those occasions am unable to due to timing, the airline never fails to disappoint. Even though their pricing may appear cheap on the onset after paying for a place to sit, and to right to check in your luggage, it often ends up more expensive then conventional airlines. Cramped seating (narrower and less legroom even compared to propellar planes) serves as a fine complement to tardiness and poor service. It boggles the mind how Air Asia has actually won airline awards! Until the Malaysian regulators intervened, they were basically running a scam where they forced credits on passengers instead of refunds, even when they cancelled flights. Some irate passengers took Air Asia to tribunals and through the legal system, fortunately justice prevailed and they rightfully got their cash refunds.

Review_Title: Case closed without refund or response
Review: I was booking a return flight from India (Ahmedabad) to Perth on 29th September. The amount was 80,832 (around $1,550 AUD). The payment was deducted from my account, but I did not receive the ticket or any confirmation. I tried again after eight hours and finally received the confirmation and return ticket. However, there was no refund or information regarding my first transaction. Two days later, AirAsia sent me another confirmation and ticket for the first payment. I raised a refund request and received an email stating that I had been given AirAsia account credit. I then requested the money to be refunded to my bank account, as this was clearly a system error. Since then, they have not responded, and the case has been closed without a refund or any further information.

Review: Worst airline experience of my life  avoid AirAsia at all costs! When I booked my ticket, it was completely unclear how much check-in luggage was included. To avoid any extra charges for overweight baggage, I arrived 3 hours early to sort everything out. The nightmare started right away. The staff were incredibly rude, dismissively telling us to just use the self-service kiosk to print luggage tags. I tried to add extra baggage through the kiosk, but it didnt work  it wouldnt even let me proceed to payment. I repeatedly went back to the staff for help, and they couldnt care less. I just kept parroting use the kiosk no matter how many times I explained it wasnt working. Eventually, I gave up and printed the labels without adding extra luggage, thinking I could talk to someone at the check-in counter. Big mistake. The staff there were even worse  rude, arrogant, and totally unhelpful. One of them even told me: You booked this ticket, not me. You have to pay 2000 MYR for extra weight. Yes  2000 MYR for some extra baggage weight! And when I explained the issue with the kiosk and how much time I lost, the response I got was: Thats your problem. Absolutely disgraceful. So what did I do? I walked away and booked a brand-new flight with Firefly the same day  got better service, polite staff, and paid less than 480 MYR for two people with 80 KG of check-in luggage. Yes, you read that right  80 KG check in plus tickets 2 persons for 480 MYR total. Same day booking. By thw way, on my flight from Kuala Lumpur to Penang, I flew Firefly and paid only 120 MYR for extra weight. Compare that to AirAsia's insane 2000 MYR demand, and its clear: DO NOT book with AirAsia. Ever. Theyre rude and incompetent, and unwillingto help. Save yourself the stress and fly with literally anyone else.

Review: My wife and I completed an online booking on 03 January 2025 for a flight from Perth to Bali on 14 August 2025 and return flight on 29 August 2025. These were paid for in full on 03 January 2025. I have now been scheduled for Open Heart surgery on 29 July 2025. Obviously the flights with AirAsia needed to be cancelled. However doing so proved to be a nightmare. If you sign up via Air Asia Move, they want to know all your personal detail, eg passport, home address, contact details for friends etc. In addition to the medical documents the process requires you to provide scanned copies of official documents in various formats, including PDF. Without completing this you cannot lodge a claim and what is most concerning is they will allow a credit, which their site states is 2 years.

Review: Consistent airline, no drama, no frills. Flew AirAsia QZ802 from Jakarta (CGK) to Bali (DPS) in May 2025. Web check-in was smooth as always with AirAsia, reliable and hassle-free. Baggage drop opened slightly later than I expected (around 4am), but otherwise efficient. Boarding was surprisingly well-organized with proper zoning enforcement by ground staff. Both CGK and DPS used aerobridges, a rare plus for an LCC. Terminal 1A in CGK was fine, though toilet access was limited. Seat was standard LCC fare, a bit cramped but fine for a short flight. AirAsias auto seat assignment has always treated me better than other airlines, often placing me at window or aisle. Cabin was mostly clean with some minor spots, and the aircraft felt slightly aged but in good condition. No in-flight entertainment, as expected. There was a schedule adjustment, but the flight not only departed early, it landed in Bali 30 minutes ahead of time! Disembarkation was smooth, and baggage claim didnt take long. Review_Title: used for flights to or from Malaysia
Review: I am extremely disappointed with AirAsia's handling of my case regarding the travel voucher. The voucher was issued due to a flight delay of more than 6 hours from Malaysia to the Philippines on October 6. Initially, the terms stated that the MYR 200 voucher was valid for two years and could be used for flights operated by AirAsia Malaysia (AK), Thailand AirAsia, Indonesia AirAsia, AirAsia Philippines, AirAsia X, and Thai AirAsia X (codes AK, FD, QZ, Z2, D7, XJ). Additionally, it was supposed to cover optional add-ons such as baggage, seats, and meals. However, I was recently informed that the voucher can only be used for flights to or from Malaysia. This was not part of the original terms when the voucher was issued, and such a restriction makes the voucher essentially useless for me as I have no plans to visit Malaysia anytime soon. To make matters worse, AirAsia has closed multiple tickets regarding this issue without providing any resolution. This pattern of ignoring customer concerns is unacceptable and reflects poorly on their commitment to customer service. AirAsia must honor the original terms they communicated. This level of service is completely unacceptable, especially when dealing with customer entitlements after such a significant delay.

Review_Title: asked me to check in my stroller
Review: Extremely disappointed with cabin crew service. Travelled with wife and toddler, with only 3 cabin baggage (2 regular size bags that can be placed under seats and 1 cabin size Quinton Roxy stroller properly packed into a cabin sized bag) from Kuala Lumpur to Singapore via AK717 on 29th September 2024. While boarding the flight, chief flight attendant stopped me and asked me to check in my stroller instead, reasoning that today's flight is full and my cabin-sized stroller is too big. Reasoned to cabin crew that I bought three seats in total and I only need one space for my stroller so that I can use my stroller after I land in Singapore immediately, without the need to travel to the baggage carousel to collect it. After all passengers boarded the plane, crew asked her colleagues to come over our seats and said that there are no more cabin space and asked us to check in our stroller. Seeing other passengers loading 2 or even 3 bags to the cabin space, I do not understand why must they target my stroller to be checked in instead of other passenger's luggages. Ultimately passed my stroller to the Crew, and gotten the assurance that I will be able to collect my stroller at the doorside area after I land in Singapore. As what we have expected, they did not pass the message to Singapore's land staff and our stroller was transported to the luggage carousel.

Review_Title: the aircraft was a total mess
Review: Rating an airline is usually about adhearance to schedule timing, crew professionalism, service - if any, and possibly pricing and on each of which AirAsia fail miserably as clearly seen in most reviews. The soundness and safety of the aircraft should not be ever a noticed deficiency, but that was the case of flight Z2 286 on Sep/25. On start, 20 minutes were added to the usual careless delay of 30 minutes, the ground and the air crews were, as usual, busy among themselves and indifferent of passengers and their needs, and the aircraft was a total mess. My seat would not stay upright, it moved backwards at will. The "biblical" excerpts glued to the back of the seats was nothing leser than forcing the Christian's religion upon others, and with that came the dripping condensation from everywhere. At destination we waited in the aircraft for 24 minutes for lack of gates. To me, flying AirAsia is the second worst thing a person would do. The discounted prices should not be a reason to accept humiliation.

So we stayed at the airport waiting to check in for another hour as we finally did. At the boarding gate, we realised that the flight might be delayed another hour the time to refuel. At 11PM, 1 hour after we were supposed to leave, we finally took off. During the flight, I asked for earplugs to help with sleeping and they said they had ran out. We landed 1 hour off schedule and that was the end of my journey with AirAsia. I highly recommend Malaysia Airlines as an alternative as they have excellent service for a couple hundred RM more.

Review: Jakarta Soekarno-Hatta to Bangkok Don Mueang. Since I did not purchase anything but a certain amount of baggage allowance, I was automatically assigned a certain seat during boarding. I was originally selected to be seated far away from my other family members, but the check-in staff members are gracious enough to make us seated not far apart. Boarding was done and completed in a timely manner, and this time, they dispatched an aircraft that had the newest batch of AirAsia seats supplied by Mirus Hawk. The seat itself, despite being slimline, was comfortable and had a few inches of extra legroom compared to the usual AirAsia seats. The only drawback was that the seat pocket was made of woven rods glued to the seat which easily became detached under some load. The cabin itself was very clean, comfortable, and in good condition overall. Take-off was on time, and there was supposedly a free Wi-Fi onboard, but it did not work. The crews did not inform it being operative as well and there was no sign saying the plane has Wi-Fi, so it's safe to say that perhaps there was indeed no Wi-Fi. The cabin crews were efficient, professional, and attentive, but I think they could improve on friendliness and some communication skills as they weren't as friendly as my previous AirAsia and Indonesia AirAsia flights. Landing was 5 minutes ahead of schedule, and my bags were delivered relatively quickly. Overall, considering that they have just defended the World's Best Low Cost Airline title, they could have done better in some aspects, but it's safe to say, I still enjoyed the flight.

Review_Title: waiting for you to make a mistake
Review: I made a mistake booking the flight. The date I booked was one month out, Aug instead of July. I did not realise the system had defaulted to Aug. I tried to contact AirAsia, but everywhere I turned on their website and Move Mobile App I was referred to "Chat to Bo" (not a real person). One of the suggestions from Bo was to reschedule the flights to my intended date using the link provide by Bo. The penalty was 125% of the price of my original ticket using his link. Making it 225% of the price of the ticket. If I were to book another ticket for the appropriate date flight, I would save 25% and keep the other ticket as well (not sure what I would do with it, though). By then it was after hours (yes, they don't have after-hour service, for an airline?). I checked the net overnight, thank goodness, they do have a "full refund if cancel within 24hr policy". Also, I checked for the procedure for the refund. It seemed there was a way but it would require further information (the all-important Order Number) from AirAsia. Easy, but not really. This information used to be provided to the customer in the form of processing the order. In mine case, this information was withheld, and I can understand. Without this number, the refund process stalled. The system does not accept my confirmed book number/code which should be adequate for all booking matters. I continued, spending many hours, looking for avenue to talk to someone or somewhere in the App to point me in the direction. Found none. Their office starts at 9am. I phoned before, right on 9am, and many times after. Before 9am, it said it was outside office hours. Right at 9am, I was connected. Yeah! But, only to be told they have "Unusually High Volume of Calls" and referred me to their website or "Chat with Bo". In the first seconds of 9am? Come on! Are they kidding me? I logged 10 calls over the next hour all with the same message. No chance to queue at all. For those interest, I logged 38 times to Move (AirAsia) website in the morning until my 24hr period of free cancellation was exhausted. While they have the cancellation policy, my only conclusion is the purpose of Policy document is to provide cover for accreditation and they certainly have no intention to allow customers accessing it. Their IT specialist must have read Catch-22 many times to come up with the algorithm to make sure me, as a customer, go round and around and come back to the starting point or to get you paying a lot of money unnecessary. There is a big difference between a customer focus quality company and one that hides in the tall grass waiting for you to make a mistake and pounce on you. Unfortunately, AirAsia is of the latter group of companies.

Review: Yogyakarta to Kuala Lumpur onboard their Airbus A320-200. Check-in at Yogyakarta was absolutely fast and we were met with friendly staff members all around. Boarding was made slightly late, but at the end of the day, boarding was completed way before the scheduled departure time. The cabin was spotless and in its best condition, and my seat, albeit tight, was decent for this 2.5-hour flight. Sadly, paperwork issues grounded our aircraft by an hour. However, once we took off, the cabin staff members immediately served meals, beverages, and duty-free items. They were friendly but could have been a little bit friendlier and more proficient in their communication skills because they were inconsistent with the announcements. Some were done in Malay only, some were done in both English and Malay. They were also a bit too stern in their tone usage. Wi-Fi entertainment was provided on this flight and it ran very fast on both my phone and my laptop. It had plenty of selections, an improvement over my previous flight with their subsidiary, Indonesia AirAsia. Landing was obviously late, but I arrived at the carousel and only waited less than 5 minutes for my bag to arrive. Overall, this was an enjoyable short-haul international flight, and after knowing this airline for almost 15 years, they haven't failed my trust.

Review: Please, I urge everyone not to fly with this airline when traveling to Bali or anywhere in Asia. Prior to booking, carefully examine their reviews. On 05/09/2024, during my flight, I encountered issues with their agent attempting to charge me $200 for a bag that was 9KG overweight. After requesting to speak with a supervisor, the price suddenly dropped to $80. Upon arriving in Bali, I noticed significant damage to one of my bags, with personal items missing from the only bag secured with a TSA lock. Despite contacting the airline for assistance, their response, received all the way from Australia, was dismissive, stating that wear and tear costs are not covered, holding me accountable for the damages. This experience has deeply disappointed me as it tainted my first trip to Bali. There has never been an airline I fly with that this has happened to me and I am urging people to not fly with this company.

Further he also helped us saved money in advising us that we could combine the weights of all our check-in luggage, so our overall ticket cost is cheaper. Review: I'm shocked and disappointed! I paid in advance on the 20th of January, and according to the bank, the payment has been successful so far. I received all the proper notifications about my upcoming flights daily. However, when I arrived at the airport, the airline informed me that there are issues with my payment and I need to purchase another ticket, which, of course, costs more. When I called my bank, they confirmed once again that my payment was indeed successful. The airline suggested that I buy another ticket now and request a refund later when I return. I am extremely frustrated for several reasons: - The airline provided incorrect information by sending me letters assuring that everything was okay, but it turned out not to be the case at the airport. The payment I made was successful, as confirmed by the bank. Why didn't the airline refund the money if the payment wasn't successful? Why should I pay more for another ticket when I already paid for the same booking? Now, in order to get a refund, I have to spend my time going to the office because they don't recognize the double payment online.

Review_Title: too late to drop the luggage
Review: Arrived at the KL airport more than 2 hours before as usual and it was a mess. Queued for several time for the check-in counter until staff told us that the check-in at the counter is extra and we need to do the self check-in. We queue for both check-in and luggage drop. Yes, two different, very slow queues because they put together all the flights. One of our luggage was 1kg too heavy and the machine didnt accept it, so we open our things and rearrange it. Once back to the machine another surprise, you cannot try again, so go queue again at the service counter. End of the story, missed flight because it was too late to drop the luggage and customer service unable to do anything outside buy another ticket  for the next day.

Review_Title: be cautious with this deceptive company
Review: The airline known as AirAsia is the most unsatisfactory flight company I've ever dealt with. They consistently come up with excuses to extract extra money from customers, even resorting to underhanded tactics to coerce additional ticket purchases, as explained in the instances below. Using AirAsia is a major warning sign; it's essential to be cautious with this deceptive company. This marks the second time in a week that I've had an incredibly unpleasant encounter with AirAsia. During the previous flight from Penang to Langkawi, they refused to check in our luggage, citing our arrival 45 minutes past the departure time. Despite being at the KLIA2 counter two hours in advance, successfully checking in our luggage, and passing the hand carry luggage check this time, they insisted on an additional 15 kg. However, at the counter, they refused to add the luggage, once again using the 45-minute rule as an excuse.The staff's impoliteness led to us missing the Miri flight, and we had to secure an alternative flight through Malaysian Airlines. This will be our last experience with the so-called air flight company, AirAsia, making it the most absurd encounter I've ever had with an airline in my life.

Review_Title: so cheap and badly put together
Review: The flight attendance were doing their best but the flight comfort itself was an absolute joke. The renting of the blankets for 2 euro or haggling over which food everyone got. Having to show boarding passes again and again to prove what you bought or didn't buy made the whole experience feel so cheap and badly put together. I don't think anyone was happy after that flight. 10 hrs flights with no entertainment is another invention I didn't think I'll experience. The flight itself wasn't even that cheap so I'm wondering why not charge 15 euro extra and give everyone all service they should get on such a long flight... the food prices were not even that expensive so I'm just sitting here n wondering why would anyone choose this uncomfortable situation for both the employees and all customers. I will not soon forget the flight attendant lady raising her voice at an older gentleman customer to tell him he can not change his order of food... it was bad. I didn't know before booking but will surely never book again. Might be ok for a short flight of 2 hrs or so but for long haul it left me with the worst taste in my mouth.

Review: I travelled by Air Asia recently, on an afternoon flight from Delhi to Kochi (I5 549). I had booked the ticket about 2-3 weeks in advance, and got a very good deal on it. The check-in was smooth. There was some delay at the gate and boarding had not started till the scheduled time for departure. Eventually we boarded about 15 Mins beyond the scheduled departure time. The take-off was quite perfect! However, the rest of the flight was smooth, except for one patch of turbulence. The plane was clean and the seat was comfortable. Even though my seat was in 24 row, leg space was not an issue. This flight was an exception. The cabin crew, especially Mr Zebran, were extremely attentive and polite. He made me feel like an honoured guest. Also the food quality was excellent. The landing at Kochi was smooth and the luggage arrived on time. Extra marks for the hospitable cabin crew! Kudos to the team and Obviously Air Asia. Will fly back soon

Review: Air Asia what a joke. Their website doesn't work. Their check in machines at the airport don't work. There is no customer service. There are no offices. There are no phone numbers to call. Worst flight I have ever taken. They say they will give you credit for your flights. Don't believe them. Your Air Asia account remains blank - no current bookings, no past bookings, no credit history, nothing in any of the folders. Make sure you have your own copy of all flight details cause you won't get them from their website or your Air Asia account. I booked online. When I received my ticket it had the wrong name, birth date and gender. What? I spent weeks hunting down a way to contact them to get it changed. Eventually I just abandoned the flight and booked on another one.

Review: First, we order a ticket that doesnt go through, the site tells us that there was an error. Suddenly we receive an email saying it anyways went through. However, the ticket suddenly had the wrong date, which we only realized when standing in the airport. We knew that we had not put the wrong date, because we both checked through everything before submitting the order, and it was further proven later when air Asia again sent a wrong date after having spoken with them on the phone. When standing in the airport in need of another ticket, AirAsia did not help us get the date adjusted unless we paid a huge amount of money. We had to pay still to pay to change our tickets, even though it was never our mistake. Afterwards, we had to call the customer service 5 times, because every time they sent an adjusted offer there was a mistake. Once the wrong flight, another time the wrong time, and then again the wrong date. Only in the end we got it right after having called 5 times. When we then fly from Krabi to Kuala Lumpur where we have to transfer, we have to pick up our luggage and check in again. We were waiting for our luggage for 30 minutes before it arrived. We then ran to the check in counter arriving 10 minutes after the gate closed and the employee did not help us. He said it was too late and we need to buy new tickets. He said that we should know the airline policy of closing the check in counter when there is 1 hour until the flight leaves and that we should have booked flights with longer time apart. I am furious and I am never flying with AirAsia again.

Review_Title: told that all 3 of us were overweight
Review: Me and my family bought tickets from Melbourne to Kuala Lumpur together with 20kg baggage for all 3 of us. I paid the tickets and the 20kg for each of us and got the email confirmation. Just after i realise that 20kg won't be enough since we have 2 check in baggage for each of us, I  purchased another 25kg for each of us. In total I purchased 45kg for each of us. To my surprise at the airport when i did my check in, i was told that all 3 of us were overweight, I had 43kg, my wife had 43.8kg and my son had 44kg. I showed the attendant at the check in counter my 2 receipts (20kg + 25kg). She said their system is showing that i have only 25kg for each of us. I explained to her that i did 2 transactions and i showed her my receipts. The explanation she gave me is that the second transaction was only for an additional 5kg even if i bought for 25kg and this is how their system works.  She charged me an additional AUD 300. Since I didn't want to miss the flight, i didn't have other options to pay but under dispute.

Review: Not for the first time have I had issues with poor staffing in the terminals, especially at Kuala Lumpur. On the way to Bangkok I was sent to the wrong gate by their concierge. Only to experience a last minute gate change which left me having to run a few kilometers or miss the flight. The worst was to come with the return to Perth flight. Having caught the Bangkok to Kuala Lumpur leg of the connecting flight I arrived at Kuala Lumpur airport with approximately 2 hrs and 15 minutes to spare. Within the Kuala Lumpur baggage claim my baggage was lost/held up and was the last to go down the carousel with one package missing. Going to lost and oversized baggage claim area the officer had to trace where my luggage was which took a very long time. That said I rushed to the AirAsia departure area and asked the customer support which line I must join for baggage drop as they have no visual clues except alphanumerical clues. I was advised to join a the W line, after 15 minutes of waiting an officer asked me where I am going to which I replied Perth, Australia. He informed that I should be in the Y line. I rushed around to the Y line, with yet another group of travellers processing massive packages, that done I get to drop my bags to be told the baggage checkin has just closed. I can not catch the connecting flight. When I asked who I may speak to in this regard I was told an officer would be present in about 3 hours. I waited for the officer and presented my case about the luggage being held up, being sent to the wrong drop off point and missing the flight. I was told it was my fault for catching those flights and that I would have to pay more for myself and my luggage for the next flight. I explained that this was an AirAsia to AirAsia connection sold by Trip.com

Review: Arrived at Gate and staff did not allow me to board simply because my passport's middle name has a dash while my Vietnam evisa does not. Told them it was very normal that you don't/can't use dash in online forms. Gate staff told me they've seen these cases many times and passengers always get denied entry, contrary to my personal experiences. So we missed the flight. It was only after boarding closed did they send me to Service Counter. Service Counter staff was superb. She simply asked Hanoi station if the evisa is valid and got a YES within 10 minutes but that good news was too late for me. Hotel rooms are non-refundable. I asked why couldn't the gate staff ask Hanoi station and the shocking answer was because "they did not have the right WhatsApp group chat". Next staff tried to get me a refund but it was rejected. The closest category was "incomplete documentation" - implying it's my fault. This is clearly the airline's fault. The Gate Staff misinterpreted visa rules and did not bother to ask proper authorities.

Review_Title: carry on bag was over weight
Review: I recently flew from Cebu to gold coast and I had to throw away a large amount of my carry on bag because it was over weight. My concern is not that I had to discard this stuff it's that the staff wouldn't let me do this and demanded I paid the extra at the gate. I watched them do this with a lot of people. I also watched them weigh guys laptop bags including mine but not ladies handbags. When I asked to check the scales calibration they were reluctant and then let me go.

Review: I tried to book directly with AirAsia and there was no way to add hold baggage online on my route Siem Reap via KUL - Sandakan, Borneo. So I booked expecting to be able to add it at the end. Then they wanted to charge 58USD per bag for a 2 hour flight. Then I tried to look up my booking on my AirAisa login and it said I do not have any booking! Then It took a awhile to find the "other airlines" bookings. They claim its booked with "another airline" not Air Asia. I tried to cancel the booking and their is no way to cancel. There is no email address, no contact number, no chat box. There is an autorobot called Bo, which gives multiple excuses as to why it cannot help you. First it says it timed out, then it said the flight is not with Air Asia but "another airline" and they have to check the rules of the other airline then it doesnt get back to you. At the airport, they restricted the baggage to 15kgs instead of 20kgs online and proceeded to charge an extortionate fee for each extra kilo. Then when we checked in they booked all of us including two children aged 9 in different seats all over the plane.

Review_Title: impossible to talk to a human
Review: Booked 7 flights with AirAsia around 4 months before my holiday. Around 2 month before the flights were supposed to take place the nightmare started. They started to cancel flights for "technical reasons" sometimes offering alternatives that were not too bad (same route, just a couple of hours earlier or later), sometimes they offered nothing. In the end of the 7 flights only 2 happened within 1h of the originally booked time. The highlight was a flight that was cancelled and begrudgingly I agreed to their alternative which was 14h earlier. After having re-arranged the other flights this affected, a couple of weeks before the flight, the alternative was moved to within 2h of the original flight's time. Even days before the flights they kept moving them around making my heart sink every time I got a message from AirAsia with the word urgent in the title. Their customer service chatbot is making it impossible to talk to a human or hand in a claim, you just go in circles with it offering you useless options. The positives were that if it was other AirAsia flights that were affected by these cancellations it was pretty painless to move them around and most of the flights were punctual.

Review_Title: Never again will we fly Air Asia
Review: For as much as I wish this was a positive letter about our experience with Air Asia, this is far from positive. And if I was able to give this airline a ZERO, I would. Plus this letter is written in behalf of my whole family of 18 who traveled to enjoy our trip to Palawan. On the morning of Sunday, April 23rd 2023, my sister in law pre-checked all of us in online prior to our arrival to the airport which she received an email confirming this check in to show at the counter. At approximately 1130 my family of 18 (included in this number was 7 children) of us traveling arrived at the Palawan Puerto Princesa Airport which was 3 hours prior to our departure back to Cebu Philippines which was around 1430, like everybody is instructed to do before every departure. At the time, they started calling for all passengers to check in their luggage for our flight, we arrived at the counter and my sister in law showed her our pre check in confirmation email. It was an overwhelming experience to our family. The policies and procedure were never clear. As the parting answer of the Manager, that the Pre-checked in option is never a guarantee that you will have a seat. My family and I took a 3 night trip to Palawan from Cebu. I, personally have traveled on numerous different airlines and never have I noticed so much unprofessionalism. And such a disappointment to most of my family members which was their first time ever being on a plane. Even though we had so much of a bad experience with Air Asia there is one police officer that show so much compassion, especially to my wife and sister in law, who were forced to stay back till they found another flight. Never again will we fly Air Asia.

Review_Title: Avoid Air Asia at all cost
Review: Avoid Air Asia at all cost. They cancelled our tickets MNL-DPS-MNL, with less than a month's notice. No other flights to or from Denpasar were available - seems to me like they have stopped flying this route. Obviously, we then had to pay twice as much to repurchase tickets so close to the departure date, and so far, 3 weeks later, we have seen no sign of an Air Asia reimbursement. This company, it turns out, is in significant financial distress (PN17 status in Malaysia), so this must be a symptom of that. Don't risk losing your money with them - Air Asia seems to have gone from hero to zero, unfortunately.

Review_Title: you pay for what you get
Review: The flight and check in was as expected, dull, nothing flash, adequate attention from check in staff and flight staff. But  I paid a small amount for the flight, so you pay for what you get. However the customer communication was awful, non existent in real terms. I needed to communicate with airasia about an amount they overcharged me. Their website and Airasia super app will only allow you to navigate a set list of questions and set answers. If your issue is outside those parameters, they are not interested. Their website and super app contains no email address or phone number to contact them about any issue out side the answers in the website. You keep getting directed to a "bot" called Ava. I managed to find on the internet 2 email addresses belonging to airasia. The response from both of them was "not our dept, Ticket closed". When asked which dept I should email, no reply. This issue has been going on 6 months now, it is not resolved. It is very clear that airasia wants nothing to do with "actual customers", I cannot comprehend how a company can still exist providing this non existent customer interaction and non existent customer servicing.

Review_Title: lousy way to treat your customer
Review: I book premium flatbed to and from KUL-Taipei, I spent over RM8000 for my wife and my tickets. We leave KUL on Christmas eve. The flight have been delayed first from 10 am to 1:30 and the gate was changed from one site of the large airport to the other side of the airport, we are asked to checked in but was usher to another room for more waiting. The flight finally left around 2:30-3:00 Pm for Taipei. At least we still have the flatbed seat we book to relax on the flight. Later on the return trip, the flight was delayed, and during checked in I realised the seat number are not consistent with my booking, so i asked the ground crew about it and was told not to worry as i still have my seat I booked .. it is a lie. We were shocked when we walk into the cabin as the configuration of the plane is different from our booking. there are no flatbed on this plane, up until now, no one have the decency to let me know about this changes. Later during the flight I asked one of the stewardess how to request for a refund for my premium flatbed booking and was told i need to make an online feedback on their website. I make the feedback after on the 3rd day after returning to KL and I didn't receive any email or message reply. last i check i have a RM400+ credit in my account which i assume is from that flight but i am not sure as i never received any response. This is a lousy way to treat your customer. I make a vow to myself not to ever fly AirAsia again.

Review_Title: the support staff were not helpful at all
Review: We had booked a flight (AK882) from Kuala Lumpur on 11.30am on 12/24/2022 to Bangkok. This flight was booked six months ago so that we can leave for Thailand after staying two days in KL. On 12/21/2022, we were notified by AirAsia via email that our flight (AK882) was cancelled due to aircraft downsize and we were re-scheduled to leave KL at 6.50am the next day (12/25/2022). Out of curiosity, we did an online search and we saw AirAsia still have other flights available from KL to Bangkok on 12/24/2022, but with higher ticket price, so we contact AirAsia support via chat and tell them that we have to be in Bangkok on 12/24 for our vacation activities and hoped they could re-schedule us to the same day flights. To our surprise, the support staff were not helpful at all by keeping on saying "no, we don't have any seat available for flights on 12/24". We ended up rebooking our flight with Malaysia Airlines to fly to Bangkok on 12/24. We are still awaiting the refunds for the canceled flight from AirAsia.

Review_Title: just don't care and disconnect you at will

Review_Title: we will avoid flying AirAsia again
Review: Check in was easy, both flights were on time and luggage arrived without incident. That was the best of it. Every announcement was screamed at us in multiple languages. They woke the whole plane up with 2.5hrs to go (scheduled landing at 0330hrs) so they could serve breakfast to about three passengers. You can't bring water on board, and you pay for it on the plane. The passengers were generally pushy and rude. KLIA is a filthy dump and we will avoid flying Air Asia again to ensure we don't have to transit there in future.

Review_Title: so nice to welcome back AirAsia
Review: It's so nice to welcome back AirAsia after almost a 2 years absence since the start of the pandemic. After having had a few bad experience with Malaysia airlines quite recently. I chose Air Asia for the value of money. On Christmas day, flight was on time. Check in at the airport was orderly, premium flex customers perks and priority were observed. Boarding by the zone was very orderly also. Plane is clean and crew were lovely and welcoming. It really pays to get the exit row for the extra leg room and comfort. My pre-ordered meal of Bukhara chicken rice was delicious ad spicy, and it came with a small bottle of water. I was impressed at how efficient the crew worked, and they kept the 2 toilets clean at regular intervals as well. Landing was so smooth too. Luggage delivered in a timely manner and in good condition. For someone who is used to flying premium airlines in business class, I must say this was a very inoressive experience. I think in future for medium to short haul flights, I really don't mind to fly with them and save some money as well. Maybe party is also because, nowadays, many premium airlines cut costs so much that, more and more, they resemble budget airlines, except for the price tag.

Review: I like Air Asia for been a good low cost airlines. But when you have an issue, the problem are starting and you will find nobody at the end for a solution or at least an answer. Air Asia changed the flight schedule by 5 hours and this was not compatible with my holidays. So I asked for a refund on Dec 2. On Dec 16, I went through the long and inefficient talk with robot AVA until I get in the queue for a human agent with n90. After an hour, I got an answer: your refund will be done in 2 days. On Dec 20, after playing again with AVA for half an hour, I was granted to the queue with number 144. And after 2 questions, the chat was terminated by the agent or ? and I still not get and answer. But every time the claim case was solved. I guess this is a great satisfaction for Air Asia to close claims, without answering their customer. In fact AVA was better than the agent, but both are inefficient. So I gave up with AVA, and called the phone voicemail service for Air Asia Thailand. I explained my case and  after 24 hours still waiting for a call back when it should have been done in 24 hours. But for urgent case I can contact AVA: joke. Form this experience, I notice that the customer service at Air Asia is non-existent, but also when you consider the price of the ticket (must check everything: seat, luggage, food), Air Asia is no more advantageous compare to a normal airlines like Malaysia Airlines or Thai. And for a small difference of the fare you can talk to a real agent instead of a stupid robot and incompetent agents. For the future I will be careful before booking at AirAsia, since this company reschedule and cancel flights many times since the start of COVID and the new normal.

Review: Although having checked in online, for the baggage drop off you are being referred to kiosk / machine to print your own luggage tag, however most of these kiosks are out of order or suddenly run into an error while processing your request. After addressing this to the staff, you're just referred back to the kiosk and after insisting it won't work, finally being referred to a counter to drop off your luggage. At waiting your turn at the counter, the completely uninterested ground staff member simple wanted to refer me back to the kiosk again even after i've stated it didn't work. When i insisted she mentioned her counter was for checking inn cabin crew only, it was clearly she was lying as other passengers were checked by her before. In the end i got another staff member involved who told the lady behind the counter to actually assist me, after which my bag was checked in smoothly within 3 minutes. in the meantime many agitated passengers encountered the same frustrating experience! I truly don't understand why an airline would tolerate such rude and indifferent behavior. I've filled a complaint with AirAsia about this, but i only received an automated answer and the day after an email saying my case was closed (with no information given or anything). Flying with AirAsia? Never again for me.

Review: I booked return flights Bandung - Singapore on 5th September 2022. I subsequently received an email from AirAsia on 15 October telling me that my flights were cancelled due to 'Operational reasons'. A promise is made for the refund to be made in 30 days. Now more than a month later, the refund is still in progress and no further advisories to enlighten me the status .... except from what I see in my account on their website. AirAsia has no customer service, no customer service phone number, not even an email address. All they have is an online chatbot named AVA, which is worse than useless. So you are on your own should any problems arise after you confirm your booking. Will definitely avoid flying AirAsia if I had a choice.

Review: Booking for onward flights were correct, there was enough time to connect in Manila. Then the airline issued a ticket under i0ne booking number and mention connection guaranteed by carrier. Booking for return flight did not alert on any issue for connection. The connecting tine (1 hour and 20 minutes) was too short for immigration formalities and taxi to next place for connecting flight. I tried to reach customer service. After hours waiting for agent i got a brief message: the company had issued 2 booking codes (one for each segment of the itinerary) so it could not guarantee the connection. I had to buy another ticket for the connecting flight, 3 hours later, to be sure I would have time to go through immigration and to take a taxi to go to departure place for connecting flight. AirAsia should not show connecting flights with too short time to connect. It should show flights with at least 3 hours for connection, or it should inform client, when booking a Bangkok to Cebu flight that client will need at least 3 hours for immigration plus transport to second place for connecting flight. It is unfair to let client book and pay flight with insufficient connecting time and then tell client "Sorry there is nothing we can do, yes you are likely to miss the connecting flight, you should buy a new ticket for a later flight".

Once again I've waited so long that the flights I wanted have disappeared and I'll now be paying a higher price for flights I didn't want. The only way to contact them now is though their terrible online chat which never understands my question. I've managed to get in an online queue but I'm number 99 and have moved one place in about 30 minutes.

Review_Title: tell me my flight its the other flight

I go to the other gate explain, they say its my fault because I didnt go to the live checkin. And had I done the online checkin properly I would have seen. This is the text on the online ticket: "you may proceed directly to the gate using this e-boarding pass". The employee said there were tickets available the same day, but until I admitted it was my fault he would not give me one for today but instead for tomorrow.  I have never ever experienced anything close to this. In the end luckily I managed to get a ticket for the same day but had to pay the difference.

Review_Title: of course I missed the flight
Review: I went to my gate entrance for my domestic flight at 2.15 when my flight is at 2.50. At the time, when the officer scanned my boarding pass the machine said "not checked in" even thought I clearly had the boarding pass. The officer said to try again in 5 minutes and the same result kept coming up until 15 minutes had passed and now the result became "too late to board". The officer told me to go to the service counter and they kept sending me to different service counters until I reached the rudest lady who initially told me that I can't board within 20 minutes of take off, even though I was at the gate 35 minutes before take off. I kept begging her to help me but all she said was "please step aside, sir. I'm busy". That was 5 minutes prior to the supposed take off and of course I missed the flight. After all of this I had to go retrieve the bags I checked in and that took over 20 minutes, at which time I found out the flight had been delayed by 40 minutes. I have to wait for the delayed flight to depart before I can take back my checked in bags. What kind of customer service is this? Also I was informed that they may help me with rescheduling the flight if I pay the difference even though 1 AirAsia employee told me it was the system's fault.

Review: I ordered my round-way tickets months in advance, only for them to say my return flight will be canceled one week before the actual flight. I needed to talk to someone to get more detail about the process, but no human is available as their system is now only a bot.  The system offered to move my return flight to another day but none of the proposed timing fit my schedule, so I asked for a refund. I was given a pending case confirmation, without any detail whatsoever on the refund processing.  Since I couldnt get any clarity, I decided to go down to the airport counter and asked some questions to the ground staff at the AirAsia counter.  She checked and confirmed the refund request was actually for both depart and return tickets, something that is not made clear in the bot chat. The ground staff could not void my refund request as this has to be done by AirAsia and not the airport staff. I had thought that the refund was only going to be for my return ticket but I had lost both tickets. Because of this, they couldnt guarantee that I will still have my name in the booking system on my departure date. The ground staff said theres nothing that can be done on their side as they have no way to reach AirAsia. Basically, I need to book another ticket which is way more expensive now that I have so little time left before my flight. So before you book your tickets think twice. It could just be a stroke of bad luck on my side, but Im reading so many reviews that are similar to mine and they help solidify why I will never book from this airline ever again.

Review: I bought a ticket through AirAsia website for a flight on the 21st of August (Sunday), from Chiang Mai to Kuala Lumpur. I received an email last Saturday, notifying me that the flight had been canceled due to "operational reasons". I tried to understand what these reasons were but I could only contact an automated bot. I eventually got to talk with a so-called live agent (had to wait in queue for 3 hours) that pretended he was typing (the 3 dots pre-message symbol showed up for like 5 minutes) and eventually the conversation "expired". I came to understand that the flight they sold me (AK 857) doesn't operate on Sundays. I have another ticket bought from them, for the 25th August (Thursday), from KL to Krabi (AK 868) and I already checked Flightaware and found out that the flight only operates on Monday, Wednesday and Friday. I asked for a refund for the flight that got cancelled by AirAsia, but so far I haven't got my money back and from the looks of it, I'm understanding that the same situation will arise with the KL to Krabi flight. Don't trust AirAsia flights and always check on several sites if the flights actually exist and operate on the days they are selling them.

Review_Title: customer service is poor beyond reasoning
Review: Purchased baggage in advance with tickets. Tried to luggage drop exactly an hour before flight time at 11.25am which was stated as 12.25pm. System error stating late. Went to the counter, and was told to queue. Spoke to a couple of other passengers and noticed they are the same flight as me, and machine also stated late and hence unable to luggage drop despite them being there way earlier than 1hr before departure. When queue reaches our turn, its 11.45pm. Counter customer service stated too late, cannot luggage drop. We were told to wait for this flight to depart first, then try to request customer service for change to another flight and pay the difference. That sounds ridiculous because the other passengers are "late" because AirAsia's own ticketing system has a problem and everyone was asked to queue until they missed the closing time for dropping luggage. I gave my luggage to my wife who drove me here and ran all the way to the departure gate. I arrived at the departure gate at 12.10pm. On the way, custom informed me that system shows I cannot board but I can try. Tried I did. To my surprise, the plane is not there yet at the departure gate when I arrived. This means Airaasia could have helped, but they simply refuse. When I tried to reason to the counter at the departure gate, all I get told was I was late. There is no sense of logic in the heads of these customer service agents. In any case, it is now 1.25pm whilst typing this review. 60 minutes delayed with no notice on estimated delay duration. There are the elderly, and kids here and they just don't seem to be bothered. When queried what is the policy for flight delay and possible cancellation, he says 2 hours first, then denies saying it back and forth. Very unprofessional and unreliable customer service. The flight status board now shows another flight, and my flight just simply gets removed without notice, causing alot of passengers crowding at the desk asking what is going on. For reference, we take AirAsia because it is cheaper sometimes. So we understand and do not complaint if it delays a little here and there. However, the customer service is poor beyond reasoning even after taking into account the slightly cheaper rates.

Review: Disappointing flight. I havent flown AirAsia for some time and seems like its punctuality and service have gone downhill. The flight was delayed by 2 hours. I wasnt informed when I checked in at the airport despite at that point the preceding flights were delayed so they should have been aware. Still no apology for being late when they stated they were "boarding" which they stated before the preceding flight had started to disembark. An hour after "boarding" they finally opened up the doors for us to get on. Still no apology, little checking of peoples tickets. Once we get on the plane its pretty clear they havent even done a cursory clean which is pretty shocking given the lessons of covid. KL T2 is not a great airport to be stuck in past the security barriers for over 2 hours. So if your flying Air Asia I recommend double checking your flight status before doing that. Or just dont fly AirAsia and maybe you wont have the same risk of long delays and poor service.

Review_Title: Why overbook a flight during a major holiday?
Review: Booked an expensive flight during Eid weekend. Checked in more than 4 hours before flight only to find out the flight is full. App told me to come to the airport early only to put me on a flight 5 hours earlier than my actual flight. No compensation, nothing. Front desk did not even tell me I would not get on the same flight. Why overbook a flight during a major holiday? Why do customers just get pushed to any flight they want without prior notice nor without compensation? Ive travelled with other budget airlines and this is just the worst. Also, customer service is bad. I cant even file a complaint. Front desk says all complaints need to go through their online chat Ava. Well, Ava is not human and doesnt understand simple keywords like refund and compensation. The worst.

Review_Title: I haven't get my refund back
Review: I've bought a return ticket from them 12th April depart, 14th April return. They cancelled the departing flight 5 days prior, the next day I got an email says that they have rescheduled my departing flight to 14th April without my consent. That makes my itinerary like departing in the morning, stay at the destination for 30minutes then returning on the same day!? I lodged a case for refund with AirAsia thru their online chat bot, as I couldn't contact any of their customer service staff. My case status was closed by AirAsia, and I haven't get my refund back. I went to the airport to check with their counter staff, they said AirAsia have refunded me. I checked with my bank, and there are not a single dime from AirAsia.  There are no way to contact their person in charge for refund. The boarding counter staffs tell you to lodge your case online with the chatbot. You can spend 2 hours and get nowhere there.

Review: An abysmal customer service experience. Trying to reach a person for help is nearly impossible, they force you to communicate solely via chat with an automated bot that is of very little help. If you are persistent, you can get through to what they lead you to believe is a human but I have my doubts. They have no interest in attempting to retain a customer in any way shape or form, the default answer is no. I was unable to make a booked flight due to rapidly changing travel plans during Covid times. I hoped I could get some kind of credit for booking another flight but they were unwilling to consider the option, which is certainly their right for the fare booked. One would figure they may have some kind of repeat customer loyalty flexibility or the ability to provide any shred of empathy, but that is clearly not the case. Lesson learned, avoid this airline at all costs.

Review_Title: This will be my last time flying with AirAsia
Review: Absolutely horrendous. Dont bother flying with AirAsia. I bought my ticket on Trip.com. I was denied boarding at the check-in counter by AirAsia, who claimed that I had cancelled my ticket on Trip.com and was already given a full refund. None of that is true. This is a non-refundable and exchangeable ticket to begin with. My ticket was still valid. I was not allowed to check in and was given zero information on whats going on. Trip.com has confirmed with me there are no refund applications made and I should be allowed to fly. I was informed by Trip.com the next day that AirAsia had rescheduled my flight for the weekend. However, I do not have any ticket issued by AirAsia and no confirmation from them. I have emailed them but received no response. I have queued for over an hour on their online customer service chat support but was kicked out of the queue because "the line is too busy". Trip.com do not have any tickets confirmation either and this entire thing is a question mark. Im just stuck in Malaysia Kuching right now with no answers, horrible service and attitude from AirAsia and a big hole in my wallet for all the money spent. AirAsia had already delayed my flight by 2 days before this situation happened. I had already rebooked and paid for another hotel, and took another pre-departure Covid test again. Now Im faced with this question mark and likely have to spend more money and time. This will be my last time flying with AirAsia. Do not even bother to consider them. Its not worth the supposedly low-ticket prices. You need to do something AirAsia.

Review_Title: I will never use this company again
Review: Booked flight in April 2020 was canceled obviously because of corona no problem. However for months trying to get either a refund or credit and it is impossible to contact a human. Their super app says that the booking number is incorrect it is what they gave me ? All you do is talk to a bot tried email no reply only phone number person said they dont do it and wouldnt give another number or any help. Left comments on their FB no reply and currently AirAsia doesnt fly to Ho Chi Minh so cant even go to airport to talk to a ticket agent. The office they had closed two years ago so right now I literally cant talk to a human to get a refund. Will just have to kiss the money goodbye, I will never use this company again, why would anyone considering if you have any problems of any kind you cant talk to a customer service rep. Obviously this company has major financial problems.

Review: Received an email notifying my inbound flight cancelled, offered following day flight which was not possible due to hotel bookings and appointments made, so I need to book another flight from another airline. Tried to get refund from AirAsia, discovered no other options except to use it's AVA robotic chat, which was not really user-friendly, and persistently keeps pushing credit rather then cash refund, I persisted, finally left on queue for agent chat, waited approx 45mins, suddenly AVA says not available, call back later! Called back later, waited another 1hr15mins, agent came on, says AA don't give cash refund even if AA cancels flights and copy pasted bits of their t&C's all over chat with link. I followed link to AA's full t&C's, which gave three options when flights are cancelled, and option C clearly stated; full refund to the bank account. So I pointed this out to agent, and copy pasted that paragraph in chat for her perusal. Agent replied; doesn't apply to domestic flights. I asked her to please show me where that is stated as I can't see it mentioned. Agent rudely left chat. I've always used AirAsia prior to this incident but now, I must say I find AirAsia unprofessional unethical business acumen is deplorable, and the unnecessarily rude attitude and rather childish mannerism of its agent totally unwarranted and uncalled for.

Review_Title: stay away from this low-cost company
Review: I travelled in Thailand with AirAsia, and this time was my worst experience ever. First, they cancelled my flight 3 times. Every time it was a few days before the flight, it cost me more than $300. They've always refused to refund me. You have to know that when you want to get a refund, no "real customer service". You can chat with a bot (which is totally dysfunctional) that always repeats the same garbage. Many people have been waiting for more than one year, never saw they money back. Second, some of their practices are very dubious. When I booked my flight, it was written "check bag included". But when I printed the ticket, I discovered it wasn't and the employee fined me for my luggage (more than 200 USD) because according to the company, it wasn't "included". Actually, when I ordered on their platform, the luggage were included, but the ticket they sent me didn't correspond to reality. I met other travelers when I was queuing up who had the same problem and were fined as well. (No refund/no exchange is possible). To conclude, you should stay away from this low-cost company which has no respect for their customers. They won't hesitate to cancel your flight, charge you extra fees and never refund you. It might be better to take a more expensive company, but more respectful toward consumers.

Review_Title: I simply want to speak to a human-being to ask them about my refund request
Review: Worst customer service ever. Last year, May 2020, our flight got cancelled due to COVID. Our refund request was validated. However, we still haven't received the refund. All other flights - different airlines - have refunded our money. A few weeks ago, there was light at the end of the tunnel - at least, we thought so - as we received an e-mail in which we were asked to provide our bank details so that we could finally get back our money. Note: it was explicitly stated that we had to reply within 7 days, which is odd as AirAsia does not reply to us at all. I have been trying to get in touch with an actual human-being for over a year now. Facebook, website chat, WhatsApp, all you get is AVA the chat bot. I provide them with my case number, my personal details and all I get is 'I am sorry, we could not find your details in our system', which is complete lies. I understand that the world has changed due to COVID, but all organizations seem to have adapted by now. I simply want to speak to a human-being to ask them about my refund request. Sure thing, I will never fly again with AirAsia. Short-term thinking, AirAsia.

Review: My flight was cancelled by Air Asia due to restrictions caused by Covid in February 2020. Now, despite not receiving a cent in refund, my case according to Air Asia, is now 'closed'. This is to inform Air Asia, that while the amount of money I have lost is not life changing, I think I speak for thousands of other former passengers cheated out of funds by you when I say that I will do everything in my power to never fly AA again. You may be desperate to stay in business but you have no right to effectively steal funds from passengers. I sincerely hope Air Asia goes bankrupt.

Review_Title: Their customer service is seriously sub standard
Review: Absolutely the worst airline to deal with! Our flights were cancelled in March due to COVID-19. I requested a refund, and on 18/03/2020 was told my refund was processed and my money would be returned within 10-15 calendar days. 8 months later and I havent received a penny! I was then emailed in May to say I could have a credit account! Replied saying are you having a laugh! No thank you! Since then I have messaged every other day via their Twitter account as this is the only way I can contact them. No phone number, no email address an infuriating automated system that tells you nothing and solves nothing. Each time I am told that my refund is in the final stage and will be back in my account shortly and nothing appears! I am a nurse in the UK and was due to go and see my dad who lives in Manila with my family. Its bad enough that our 3 week holiday was cancelled, this is just rubbing salt in the wound! 500 of my hard earned money that they were quick enough to take is still not back with me! I couldnt be angrier. Their customer service is seriously sub standard and I would not recommend anyone to use this company! Modern day robbery at its finest. Would be giving 0/10 if allowed, but has to begrudgingly be a 1/10! Seriously, avoid this company like the plague.

I requested a refund, and on 18/03/2020 was told my refund was processed and my money would be returned within 10-15 calendar days. 8 months later and I havent received a penny! I was then emailed in May to say I could have a credit account! Replied saying are you having a laugh! No thank you! Since then I have messaged every other day via their Twitter account as this is the only way I can contact them. No phone number, no email address an infuriating automated system that tells you nothing and solves nothing. Each time I am told that my refund is in the final stage and will be back in my account shortly and nothing appears! I am a nurse in the UK and was due to go and see my dad who lives in Manila with my family. Its bad enough that our 3 week holiday was cancelled, this is just rubbing salt in the wound! 500 of my hard earned money that they were quick enough to take is still not back with me! I couldnt be angrier. Their customer service is seriously sub standard and I would not recommend anyone to use this company! Modern day robbery at its finest. Would be giving 0/10 if allowed, but has to begrudgingly be a 1/10! Seriously, avoid this company like the plague.

Review_Title: big shout out to the AirAsia staff
Review: My sister and I had booked an AirAsia flight from Phuket to Bangalore which had a 11-hour layover in Bangkok so that we could visit Bangkok for a few hours. Review: Singapore to Kuala Lumpur. Even after adding in seat selection and 20kg of baggage, the price was still affordable and cheap considering that I only booked a month before the flight. There were problems with the self check-in as I had checked in online with AirAsia, luckily this was sorted out very quickly. Immigration at Changi Airport was very fast. The plane left on the dot at 7:35am and the gate my plane was at was the closest to the runway, so we were in the air very quickly. The seat pitch was just about okay for my height, and it was comfortable for a short flight. The plane was one of the older ones in AirAsia's fleet, not fitted with the sharklets seen on the newer A320s, and the cabin did show signs of ageingty. As it was a 40min flight and a budget airline, there was not much the cabin crew had to do but they were warm and friendly. One interesting perk about this flight was the ability to stream the inflight entertainment into my own device without needing to download a seperate app; all I had to do was to connect to the inflight WiFi which was enabled even on this short hop and I found it very unique, setting aside AirAsia from other budget carriers, and the selection was quite generous too, with some movies, documentaries and even games. The flight landed in Kuala Lumpur early and we had a short taxi into KLIA2, very quickly we were disembarking. Overall, given AirAsia's punctuality and cost, I would definitely pick AirAsia again if I'm travelling to Malaysia or want to travel on a budget.

Review: Tagbilaran to Manila. AirAsia is extremely gimmicky and unprofessional! I've flown with very many different airlines for almost 40 years now and I've never ever experienced such unprofessional treatment. They are the only airline that counts your personal/cosmetic bag as part of carry-on baggage weight. However, they only selectively enforce this policy so that makes them utterly unprofessional. I chose to fly with AirAsia for the first time because they had the cheapest cost for my round trip from Manila to Tagbilaran/Panglao, Bohol in the Philippines. On the Manila, to Tagbilaran flight, my carry-on baggage and personal/cosmetic bag were not even checked or weighed at all, but on my flight back the customer service agent chose to enforce their stupid policy and made me pay an extra baggage fee. The worst part about this experience was that I was made to open and repackage my personal/cosmetic bag, carry-on bag, and checked baggage while I was at the check in counter. I was struggling to get the weight just right for them and a security guard noticed me struggling and whispered to me to just take out my laptop from my carry-on baggage because then AirAsia won't count it as carry-on weight if I only take out my laptop and carry it by hand. It's so damn ridiculous! Why didn't the customer service agent just tell me this and save me money, time, embarrassment, and frustration in the first place? Why did she make me go through all that trouble and money and time wasted? I also asked this customer service agent to let me speak with the manager and she just kept telling me "ok" or "they're busy right now." I also noticed that very few other passengers were made to weigh both their carry-on and personal/cosmetic bags as I were. This horrible airline is the worst airline I've ever flown on in my almost 40 years of flying all over the world on so many other different airlines and I will never ever fly with this joke of an "airline" ever again. If you want professional and fair service do not ever fly with Air Asia! They're just a gimmick and a huge joke!

Review_Title: Still waiting to board, holiday ruined
Review: Kuala Lumpur to Nha Trang with AirAsia. Simply the worst airline in the world, many years running. Ive been unfortunate enough to have to rely on this sorry excuse for an airline for years and not once, not once, have I ever had a good experience. Not a single flight that went smoothly, with no delay or some other nonsense. Awful service, always late, anything to take your money and always unpleasant. The only thing they havent charged us for yet is oxygen on board, which at some point it will probably be charged also, 10 cents per breath. If only there were other options for certain destinations. Todays misfortune: retiming of flight the day before, only to come to airport, go to gate as indicated and find out there (i.e. at the gate) that there would be another delay on top of a change in gates. Still waiting to board, holiday ruined. Avoid this airline if you can. Pay more, get better service somewhere else.

Review_Title: Worst service I have ever experienced
Review: Phuket to Chiang Mai with AirAsia. Worst service I have ever experienced on an aircraft. I'm 6'4" tall and literally could not sit in my seat without physically bruising my knees as the legroom is that short. I stood for entire flight apart from when seatbelt signs were on and came away with bad bruising to both my knees. Despite there being 11 extra legroom seats available the cabin crew would not let me sit there (even for extra payment that I offered) but instead just smiled at me as I stood. I showed then the damage the seat had done to my knees after take off and they just did not care. I watched them throw a lady out of the extra legroom seat who was trying to breastfeed a baby, this is how uncaring they are. On making a subsequent complaint I received a totally inadequate reply from them. If you are over 5'8" do not use. I am now looking at taking legal action for my injuries and suffering as only way I can see these airlines learning.

Review_Title: do not travel with Air Asia
Review: If you're a person with a disability, do not travel with Air Asia from Don Mueang Airport. You will have to climb the stairs to get on the plane. Even if you pay 600 Thai bahts for the wheelchair service, this stops at the departure gate! A family member or friend can do it. I guess they don't want to pay for direct access to their aircraft? Of course the bus does not stop at the edge of the platform, you'll have to get down a high step and then get on the bus, one more step. Then get off the bus, one more step and a nice stair is waiting for you and your disability! In fact, Air Asia has changed its slogan: "Now the disabled can walk" And Don Mueang airport is changing its name, it is now the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Lourdes Airport! The place where miracles happen, in this case, it's a paid miracle, a miracle that will never happen.

Although I was flying on cheap tickets I could buy exit row aisle seats for both flights. The middle seat remained vacant in both flights probably because no one wants to pay for the pleasure of sitting in the middle seat! In both flights the meal that I had preordered turned out to be pretty tasty. This is what I like about this airline - at the time of booking you can buy the degree of comfort that you want, verify it and then pay for it. Many other airlines would not allow cheap ticket holders to select premium seats. After flying full service airlines on business for decades I have recently switched to AirAsia in part due to the simplicity of the whole process - booking, seat and meal selection, checkin and boarding.
